text.title: AI-Assisted Accessibility Workflow
text.summary: 5-stage pipeline from automated scan to user validation, including the human-AI responsibility split and ROI data.

list.item: 1. Automated Scan: run axe, WAVE, Lighthouse; AI prioritizes findings; false positives filtered (10–15% remain)
2. AI Suggestions: review AI-proposed code fixes; understand why issue matters; get multiple fix options ranked by best practice
3. Manual Verification: test keyboard navigation; verify logical fix makes sense; check edge cases AI missed
4. User Testing: test with people with disabilities using real assistive technology; validate AI assumptions
5. Continuous Monitoring: AI scans on every deploy; track trends over time; alert on new critical issues

rule.statement: AI handles repetitive scanning and report generation; humans handle all contextual decisions and user testing validation.
rule.rationale: AI finds issues faster and more consistently; humans provide context, empathy, and judgment. Combining both produces 85–95% issue coverage vs 50–70% for manual alone.

list.item: AI: repetitive scanning, code extraction, report generation, WCAG Q&A, draft alt text, priority ranking, regression testing
Human: context and empathy in content decisions, complex judgment calls, user testing, strategic fix decisions, edge cases, final review, accessibility culture
Manual only: 50–70% issue detection; AI + Manual: 85–95%
Continuous AI: prevents 40% of issues before production

rule.statement: Scan new pages on every deployment, not just periodically — regressions appear silently.
rule.rationale: New code regularly introduces accessibility regressions. One-time audits miss regressions introduced between audits. CI-integrated scanning catches them at the source.

text.reference: Haiku prompt for axe JSON issue triage and ranking
